core_mechanism: Synchronous (lock-in) demodulation: correlating a weak periodic signal against a reference waveform locked to the signal's frequency and time-integrating to pass a narrow spectral band while rejecting out-of-band noise, with the reference reshaped into a smooth triangular (piecewise-linear) modulation function to suppress the harmonic spectral leakage that a square-wave reference introduces.
brief_reason: The abstract frames the method as lock-in detection whose signal-to-noise ratio is limited by 'spectral leakage' from pulsed (square-wave) control, and cures it by implementing triangular modulation functions, i.e., waveform-shaping the reference of a phase-sensitive synchronous demodulator to eliminate leakage.
